meta.name: Preposition Case Rules
meta.description: Rules for German prepositions: which case each governs, with notes for two-way (Wechsel) prepositions and special cases. Used by the Grammar Rules browser and the verb+preposition review hints.

categories.two-way: Wechselpräposition — Akk for direction, Dat for static location; with fixed-preposition verbs the verb sets the case

an.rule: an is a two-way preposition. Locally it takes Akk for direction, Dat for static location — but with fixed-preposition verbs the case is set by the verb.

auf.rule: auf is a two-way preposition. Locally it takes Akk for direction, Dat for static location — but with fixed-preposition verbs the case is set by the verb.

in.rule: in is a two-way preposition. Locally it takes Akk for direction (into), Dat for static location (in) — but with fixed-preposition verbs the case is set by the verb.

über.rule: über is a two-way preposition. Locally it takes Akk for direction (over), Dat for static location — but with fixed-preposition verbs it almost always takes Akk (e.g. sprechen / nachdenken / sich freuen über + Akk).

unter.rule: unter is a two-way preposition. Locally it takes Akk for direction, Dat for static location — but with fixed-preposition verbs the case is set by the verb (often Dat: leiden unter + Dat).

vor.rule: vor is a two-way preposition. Locally it takes Akk for direction, Dat for static location — but with fixed-preposition verbs it almost always takes Dat (Angst / sich fürchten / warnen vor + Dat).

zwischen.rule: zwischen is a two-way preposition. Locally it takes Akk for direction, Dat for static location — but with fixed-preposition verbs it usually takes Dat (unterscheiden zwischen + Dat).

hinter.rule: hinter is a two-way preposition. Akk for direction, Dat for static location — with fixed-preposition verbs, the verb sets the case.

neben.rule: neben is a two-way preposition. Akk for direction, Dat for static location — with fixed-preposition verbs, the verb sets the case.

als.rule: als is a comparison particle, not a true preposition. After verbs like halten / ansehen / bezeichnen + Akk + als, the noun after 'als' agrees with the object's case (Akk).
